skill.name: hig-platforms
skill.description: Apple Human Interface Guidelines guidance for platform-specific product and interface design across iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, visionOS, watchOS, and Apple games.
skill.summary: Apply Apple HIG platform conventions across Apple apps and games.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many potential issues, but review found the high-risk items were false positives from Apple documentation text and image hashes. The package contains markdown guidance only, with no executable scripts, network calls, credential handling, or prompt injection found. Remaining risk is low because the skill links to external Apple resources and asks the agent to read a local project context file if present.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The detected URLs are markdown source links, image links, and Apple Developer references. They do not execute code or make runtime network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review shows static URLs embedded in markdown documentation and attribution links. No code path performs fetch, curl, or other outbound requests.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the line only formats a local markdown path. There is no command invocation or executable script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line contains `.claude/apple-design-context.md` as inline markdown text. It is not shell syntax in an executable file.

low_findings.title: Optional Local Context File Read
low_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to check a local project context file before asking questions. This is useful context handling, but it should remain limited to user-approved workspace files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction clearly requests reading a local path if present. No evidence shows exfiltration, secret harvesting, or access outside the workspace.

content.value_statement: Designing one app across Apple platforms can create mismatched interactions. This skill provides HIG-based guidance for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, visionOS, watchOS, and games.
actual_capabilities: Compare design conventions across iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, visionOS, watchOS, and games.
Recommend platform-specific navigation, input, layout, and interaction patterns.
Explain when to adapt an interface instead of copying it between platforms.
Reference Apple HIG platform guidance and related resource pages.
Produce platform difference tables and implementation notes for app teams.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not generate production UI code.
It depends on bundled markdown references that may lag behind Apple updates.
It does not replace usability testing on real devices.
It focuses on platform behavior, not branding or visual identity systems.

use_cases.title: Plan a Multi-Platform App
use_cases.description: Map one product concept to the right navigation, input, and layout patterns for each Apple platform.

use_cases.title: Adapt an Existing iPhone App
use_cases.description: Identify what should change when bringing an iPhone app to iPad, Mac, Apple TV, Apple Watch, or visionOS.

use_cases.title: Review Game Platform Fit
use_cases.description: Check whether a game respects Apple platform expectations for controls, accessibility, display, and system features.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Platform Patterns
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new Apple app.
prompt_templates.prompt: I am designing a new [app type] for [platforms]. Recommend platform-specific navigation, input, and layout patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Platforms
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare how this feature should work on iPhone and iPad: [feature]. Include layout, input, navigation, and accessibility differences.

prompt_templates.title: Port an Existing Experience
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced adaptation from one Apple platform to another.
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an existing [source platform] app with [core workflows]. Explain how to adapt it for [target platform] without copying patterns directly.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Platform Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Apple platform design plan for HIG fit: [design summary]. Return risks, platform-specific changes, and implementation notes.

output_examples.input: Design a task manager for iPhone, iPad, and Mac.
output: iPhone should prioritize tab navigation, compact task entry, and one-handed actions.
iPad should use a sidebar, split views, keyboard shortcuts, and pointer support.
Mac should expose menu commands, resizable windows, toolbar customization, and denser information layouts.

output_examples.input: Review a visionOS meditation app concept.
output: Use standard windows for setup and preferences.
Reserve immersive spaces for guided sessions where immersion adds clear value.
Keep controls within a comfortable field of view and support indirect gestures.

output_examples.input: Adapt a game from console-style controls to Apple platforms.
output: Provide touch controls for iPhone and iPad while supporting physical controllers where available.
Use focus navigation for tvOS and keep text legible from across the room.
Avoid assuming every player can use a hardware controller.

best_practices: Start with the user context and primary input method for each target platform.
Translate the workflow intent instead of copying layouts between platforms.
Pair recommendations with accessibility, ergonomics, and system feature checks.
anti_patterns: Do not use one iPhone layout unchanged across iPad, Mac, tvOS, watchOS, and visionOS.
Do not ignore platform input differences like touch, pointer, remote, gaze, and Digital Crown.
Do not treat Apple HIG references as a substitute for testing on target devices.

faq.question: Which Apple platforms does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, visionOS, watchOS, and Apple game design guidance.

faq.question: Can it create SwiftUI or UIKit code?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance, comparison tables, and implementation notes, not production code.

faq.question: Does it replace Apple Human Interface Guidelines?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes and applies bundled HIG references for faster platform-specific reasoning.

faq.question: Is it useful for multi-platform planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It is strongest when comparing how one workflow should change across Apple platforms.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: No runtime internet access is required. The bundled references include external Apple documentation links.

faq.question: What should users provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide target platforms, app type, core workflows, current design assumptions, and any platform constraints.
